meta.scene_summary: We are busy rewriting everything in Rust while the EU rewrites our right to privacy.

cooking.headline: Postgres in Rust is officially the 'I'll rewrite it myself' final boss
cooking.summary: Someone finally took the developer mantra of rewriting everything in Rust and actually finished the job for the world's most reliable database. It turns out that passing 100% of the regression tests is the ultimate way to tell your coworkers that your new side project is no longer a toy.
cooking.plain_english: A developer has successfully rewritten the PostgreSQL database engine using the Rust programming language while maintaining full compatibility with original system testing standards.

cooking.headline: The EU is trying to install a global 'send to all' for your private DMs.
cooking.summary: Lawmakers want to scan your encrypted messages for illegal content, effectively turning 'end. to.
cooking.plain_english: The European Union is considering new legislation that would require messaging services to scan private user messages for illegal material.

cooking.headline: John Deere finally admits that owning a tractor shouldn't require a software license.
cooking.summary: After years of treating tractors like iPhones that need a Genius Bar appointment, John Deere is finally letting owners fix their own gear. It turns out that 'proprietary software' is just a fancy way to prevent you from using your own wrench.
cooking.plain_english: John Deere has reached a settlement that allows farmers and independent repair shops to access the tools and software needed to fix John Deere equipment.

cooking.headline: PostHog is going open source so you can finally self-host your own data-tracking existential crisis
cooking.summary: PostHog is making the jump to open source, letting you peek behind the curtain of your own analytics stack. It's like being given the keys to the surveillance van you've been riding in all along.
cooking.plain_english: PostHog has released its software under an open source license, allowing anyone to inspect, modify, and host the code themselves.

cooking.headline: SWE-1.7 is now smart enough to be your senior dev's replacement
cooking.summary: Cognition's latest model has reached the intelligence of industry-leading giants, which is great for productivity and terrible for my job security. It's basically the equivalent of the kid in class who finishes the exam in ten minutes and gets a perfect score while you're still reading the instructions.
cooking.plain_english: The software engineering AI model SWE-1.7 has achieved performance levels comparable to the most advanced AI models currently available.

cooking.headline: Microsoft wants you to visualize why your AI agent is hallucinating
cooking.summary: Microsoft released Flint so you can finally map out exactly where your AI agent decided to take a hallucination-fueled detour. It's like a debugger for your code, but for the moments when your bot stops working and starts making things up.
cooking.plain_english: Microsoft has introduced a new visualization tool called Flint designed to help developers track and understand the decision-making processes of AI agents.

cooking.headline: AI makes rewriting your entire codebase as easy as hitting delete
cooking.summary: We used to fear rewrites because they required actual effort and months of debugging, but now AI makes the process cheap enough to do on a whim. It's like having a printer that can churn out a whole new project while you just watch the ink levels drop.
cooking.plain_english: Artificial intelligence tools are changing the financial decision-making process for companies choosing between maintaining legacy software or building it from scratch.

cooking.headline: The leap second is taking the rest of the year off
cooking.summary: Engineers everywhere can breathe a sigh of relief knowing they don't have to battle time-sync bugs during their holiday break. It's the rare occasion where doing absolutely nothing is actually the most productive thing we could do.
cooking.plain_english: International authorities have decided not to add an extra leap second to the clock at the end of 2026.

cooked.headline: EU Chat Control is the digital equivalent of a TSA agent reading your diary
cooked.summary: The EU has greenlit scanning private messages to catch bad actors, effectively turning every encrypted app into a digital TSA checkpoint. It's a bold strategy to secure the house by installing cameras in every single bedroom drawer.
cooked.plain_english: The European Parliament has approved legislation that allows for the automated scanning of private digital communications to detect illegal content.

cooked.headline: Sony thinks your digital library is a gym membership you only use on leap years
cooked.summary: Sony is reportedly setting an expiration date on your digital games if you don't log in for three years. It turns out you don't actually own your games, you're just renting them until the company decides you've moved on.
cooked.plain_english: PlayStation may remove access to purchased digital games if the user account has been inactive for three years.
